steps.title: Identify the critical gap
steps.content: The passage marks a turning point with explicit contrast: “While neither History nor any works that predate it mention Arthur's famous Round Table, Le Morte d'Arthur does.” The question is asking: why does Malory's text include something that Geoffrey's and earlier sources don't?

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C answers directly: Malory found the Round Table in a source that Geoffrey of Monmouth never encountered, explaining why the detail appears in Le Morte d'Arthur but not in History or earlier texts.

Choices A and B both assume Malory worked exclusively from Geoffrey (which would exclude the Round Table), while D makes an irrelevant similarity comparison.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
